title: Recruiter Screens, Written Communication, and Process Control · Opportunity Brief: Screen Close
summary: A fast screening call mixes role interest, evidence, compensation, location, authorization, accommodations, private history, and unclear next steps. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the screen close increment in screening conversation and process record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.
Request process details and needed access adjustments with learner-controlled disclosure and location-specific rights awareness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Screen Close · Theory Screen Close
steps.instruction:
theory case — A rushed screen mixes role evidence, pay, location, authorization, access, sensitive history, unclear process, and an implied but unconfirmed next step. The theory variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Screen Close contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Screen Close · Theory Screen Close.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record. For Predict Screen Close · Theory Screen Close, keep Screen Close connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A rushed screen mixes role evidence, pay, location, authorization, access, sensitive history, unclear process, and an implied but unconfirmed next step. content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Screen Close condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: FTC Job Scam Consumer Alert — Controls recruiter identity verification, premature sensitive-data requests, payment and equipment-check fraud, urgency signals, and independent employer-channel confirmation.

hints: For Predict Screen Close · Theory Screen Close, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Screen Close precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Predict Screen Close · Theory Screen Close, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A rushed screen mixes role evidence, pay, location, authorization, access, sensitive history, unclear process, and an implied but unconfirmed next step. The theory variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. For Predict Screen Close · Theory Screen Close, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: A friendly conversation implies advancement.

options.text: A friendly conversation implies advancement. That claim fails the predict screen close evidence boundary for Screen Close.

For predict screen close, apply Screen Close without its stated preconditions, omit the screening conversation and process record trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Screen Close for screening conversation and process record.
Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Screen Close while producing screening conversation and process record.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A rushed screen mixes role evidence, pay, location, authorization, access, sensitive history, unclear process, and an implied but unconfirmed next step. The worked example variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record. A defensible solution applies Screen Close, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to screening conversation and process record.

content.code:
# Worked focus: career-screen-close — Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-4
focus: career-screen-close
decision-evidence: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A friendly conversation implies advancement. This fails the Screen Close contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: FTC Job Scam Consumer Alert (Published 2025-07-10; reviewed 2026-07-15) — Controls recruiter identity verification, premature sensitive-data requests, payment and equipment-check fraud, urgency signals, and independent employer-channel confirmation.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.

stimulus.title: Screen Close failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Screen Close, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

Keep the Screen Close artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the screen close defect evidence boundary for Screen Close.

For repair the screen close defect, apply Screen Close without its stated preconditions, omit the screening conversation and process record trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Screen Close for screening conversation and process record.

steps.title: Defend the Screen Close model · Theory Screen Close
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Screen Close: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A rushed screen mixes role evidence, pay, location, authorization, access, sensitive history, unclear process, and an implied but unconfirmed next step. The theory variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.

Evidence target: Defend the Screen Close model · Theory Screen Close.
steps.why: Retrieving Screen Close through the concrete screening conversation and process record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the Screen Close model · Theory Screen Close, keep Screen Close connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.
